title: Hey guys, i've finally done it!
author: No_Page5992
selftext:
Hey everyone,

if you'd look on my profile, we were busy on creating a bot which would be operational all by itself.  
Unfortunately, this did not work out the way we'd expect it to go.  
We went back to the drawing table and came to the conclusion that almost any strategy would need some sort of human discretion, so we took this with us to our next strategy to automate.

This, after some time and updates has worked out pretty impressive.  
This bot is not meant to replace the trader, but rather enforce it.  
In simple terms, the only thing i now need to do, is analyse the higher timeframe and ask myself ''Do i anticipate price to go up or down?''  
If i can't answer this, i simply leave the bot turned off, and if i can, i set the bot up for only buys/sells.  
That's literally it, i can do this the night before the next day, and the bot will run flawlessly the next day.  
  
I've been using the bot myself now, and almost passing my second FTMO account using this bot.

Now, i will engage with a true test to see whether this bot is actually as good as i envision it at the moment.  
I contacted 3 friends of mine from back in the day in the army.  
1 of them is a trader, 2 are not.  
I will send them a code-less copy to use, and have a zoom-meeting to explain how to operate the bot, what the bot does and some basics related to trading.

If these guys can make money using this bot, or simply pass a funded account, with little to literally 0 experience in trading overall.  
I'd say this bot is a enormous success.

If this is the case, i will also start selling this bot.  
Not only selling, but i will also create a Discor. Group with all the buyers and me inside.  
This to share experiences, share bugs and/or performance related issues, things people might need extra etc..  
Or simply for sharing each others analysis to gather the best results possible for literally everybody.  
I believe that within a group, we achieve way more then alone.  
But that is my vision.

I do NOT believe the nonsense ''when too many people use a strategy it'll get patched''  
I trade gold myself, if people believe this, they should look up how much money goes through the gold market each single day 

Now, thank you guys for reading, and let me know what you guys think, or might even have tips how to do things differently, or better! :)  
Thank you!

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body: Will do! :)

comments.body: How many tickers are you following?

Only 1!
Xauusd's volatility is what this automation needs to find success.

Although i have not yet tested this abroad many other instruments, as i only trade gold myself as well, but these tests will be conductef for sure later on.

comments.author: onetimeengineer
comments.body:
Interesting. Thanks for sharing your experience. 

I created my own autonomous trading system with the goal of full automation unless there's an exception that requires my attention. https://www.zephyrapex.com/ 

It's currently in alpha testing with equity paper trading, but I'm aiming to configure real equity and crypto accounts next month after reviewing all the alpha-testing lifecycle data.

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
I tried creating a full automated system for years.
This is extremely hard to achieve, but adding just a little human discretion to a automated system like i did now, increases the profitability by an extreme margin.

Now, i have a system that even a toddler could operate, only needing +-5 min of your attention each day.

My advice? 
Try something like this, instead of going down that never-ending hole for full automation.

Good luck to you buddy !!

replies.author: onetimeengineer
replies.body: Thanks. I believe if the institutions can do it with their algos, I can do it.

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
Exactly!
It is possible, why can't we.

replies.body: looks solid! I would love to test it out! :)

I'll baseline and reconcile at the end of this month. I'll then decide if I need another clean month of paper trading before I risk some real money.

comments.author: ddchbr
comments.body: Wha about the HTF direction bias do you need to do manually? Is determining this quite an advanced process? I code trading strategies and such and I would be happy to discuss solving for this.

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
Hey!

First of all, thank you for your reply, and giving us the opportunities you provide.
We use certain momentum and volatility indicators to determine numerous things, which is also one of the bots entry criterea.

For example, we use a momentum oscillator to determine the bots bias upon our own bias.
But this oscillator can sometimes be incorrect, for example when price keeps rising or dipping for extensive periods of time.
The indicators work perfect in healthy trends, they work less perfect in extreme movements.

This is where our human discretion comes in to place, and why this bot is extremely profitable.
We decide with our own eyes and logical thinking.
"Is this a healthy location for price to be in, does it align with what the bot would do" etc..

I intend to keep it this way, as there is nothing that exists yet that could replace this kind of sense.
It's hard to explain non-visually, but this is by far the best option we have.
Anyone can make the time free for 5m a day, and it is extremely simple to do.

Anyhow, thank you, very much!

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
PS:

The process of determining whether to turn the bot off/on is never mechanical.
It requires logical but simple thinking.

This is why it's irreplacable right now.

comments.author: topicspace
comments.body:
This is a really interesting direction — especially the part where you realized full automation breaks down and you need a human layer for bias/direction.

What’s interesting is *what that human decision actually is*.

A lot of the time it’s not really “price up or down” — it’s a judgment about whether the **underlying story is strengthening or not**.

Like:

* is this move backed by something that’s reinforcing?
* or is it just noise / reaction / short-term flow?

I think that’s where a lot of bots struggle — they execute well, but they don’t have a good way to distinguish:

* real narrative formation
* vs fragmented / conflicting signals
* vs high-attention noise

Feels like your setup is basically:  
**human = narrative filter**  
**bot = execution + discipline**

Curious how you’re deciding direction right now — is it mostly technical, or more macro / news / context driven?

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
Yes!  
You got it right.  
We have made numerous bots, based on profitable strategies WHEN performed my human.  
But they always seem to fall apart when being automated.  
Often, people skip their human discretion in their strategy, but use it automatically, without often realising themselves.

This is something we've found out after countless of failures.  
This example we have here, is close to perfection IMO.  
We will see next week, once people start using it with zero knowledge about trading.

My directional bias is still purely technical.  
And the crazy this is, it doesn't need to be perfect. A simple and logical choice is already enough to be able to set the bot up for that desired direction.

For example, my entire bias was wrong last friday, but the bot still made a profit.  
Won 2 trades, lost 1. 1RR trades.  
Usually it takes more trades, when bias is correct.

This is because it waits for strong momentum in my anticipated direction on the execution timeframe, being the 1 minute.  
Even after this momentum, it still waits for certain confirmation that this momentum is truly a move price wants to go.

Thank you for your interest!

comments.author: Prabuddha-Peramuna
comments.body:
Since you asked for some honest feedback, I’ll give it to you straight.

You mentioned that "literally the only thing" you do is decide if the price is going up or down. I’d be careful with that wording. In the professional world, picking the daily bias is the hardest part of the job. If you can get that right consistently, you already have the "edge." The bot is just an execution tool. 

The Army Friends Test - 

Testing this with three buddies (especially two non-traders) is a double-edged sword.if they catch a lucky gold rush week, they’ll think they’re geniuses and you’ll think the bot is invincible. If they hit a choppy range and blow their accounts, you’ve just lost money for your friends.A true test needs hundreds of trades across different market regimes. Three people isn't a sample size; it’s a liability. I’d suggest running a verified **Myfxbook** or a **Track Record** first. In this industry, "trust me" doesn't sell but data does.

The Sales Red Flag -

If you mention selling the bot before you've even fully passed a single FTMO account yourself, people are going to label it a "scam" immediately. The logic most traders use is: *"If the bot can print money on a $200k funded account, why does he need my $50 for a Discord sub?"* My advice? **Pass the accounts first.** Get the payouts. Get the certificates. Once you have the proof of withdrawals, you won't even have to "sell" itpeople will be begging you for the link.

The Liquidity Argument -

You’re right that Gold is a massive market, but be careful saying it can’t be "patched." While the market won't stop working, your **broker's execution** might. If 1,000 people are all using the exact same bot to enter at the exact same millisecond, you’re going to run into massive slippage and "A-book/B-book" issues with prop firms.

You've got a great concept with the "human + machine" approach. But if I were you, I'd keep the code to myself for another 3–6 months, stack some real withdrawals, and then come back to the community with a "verified track record" instead of a "vision." It’ll save you a lot of headaches.

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
Hey!

I appreciate you, and your critical thinking.
Thank you for that.

Maybe i've worded some things wrong, so let ne elaborate things further.
On the first note, i see and understand all that you have said.

First of all, i did say i almost passed my second account.
Which means i already passed one.
This is only using the bot, ive been trading this strategy the bot uses for a longer period of time, and passed more accounts doing so.

About my final test, yes.
Yes ofcourse, luck could always play a role here, but thaf is why i have chosen 3 people.
They will all do different things, of course it is up to me to determine whether that is luck, or not.

Lastly, this automation would not be cheap, it's not for everyone hahaha.
Again, thank you!!

replies.body: You are welcome

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
Hey bud!
First of all, thank you!
I already have a price that i'm selling it for.
I sell it to anyone who wants it.
Once demands starts to grow, the price will grow with it.

Including the price, you'd have a call with me in which i explain all the inside outs, the reasoning behind the engineering, and how to operate.
Also i'm busy right now actually in building a group, in which all the buyers can gather to share all kinds of things, analysis of one and other, actual trades people have gotten from the bot, questions they can ask me or my team, or even ask for certain improvements that they'd like the bot to have.
A extra risk parameters for example.
Updates will be included in the price as well of course.

I do this so also people with very little knowledge avout trading, have no need to feel 'lost' after buying this.
This way, everyone and anyone will be able to get the most out of this.
Also, IMO, as a group we always stand stronger.
Maybe someone of experience, finds something while using the bot that limits possible profits for example, this helps us all in the end.

comments.author: Additional_Round6721
comments.body:
honest question  what happens if price moves against your bias mid-session and the bot keeps running?
had this exact problem. bot ignored its own rules after a restart, kept trading markets i'd already closed. lost before i caught it.
curious how you handle that edge case before you scale it to your friends

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
Great question!

The human addition to this system is simple, but effective.
We only need to look at the markets and decide, is this location logical for price to rise or fall?
And turn the bot on for that direction.

Were you bullish, and the market falls like a rocket?
Most of the times the bot will simply NOT trade, as the strategy has multiple layers of confirmation, each of them need a certain momentum in our direction.
If this doesn't happen, there will be no trade.

Sometimes price can fall but still have strong pullbacks.
This exact thing happened to me last friday myself.
I was bullish on gold, so i turned the bot on for only buys.
Price dipped pretty hard, but i still made a profit!
The bot took 3 trades, which 2 of them were wins.

You can DM me for proof, if you'd like ofcourse.

replies.author: Additional_Round6721
replies.body:
that friday example is actually a good stress test. most people would've turned it off and missed the pullback trades
good luck with the friends trial, curious how the non traders perform.

replies.body:
Yes!!
Friday was actually very cool to see, and made me even more optimistic.
And yes, how the non traders perform will be deciding, whethet i can start the sale in a few weeks, or not.

Thank you!!🤙
